subject: Docking map thin-slot analysis — 3 bugs identified
body:
## Thin-slot bugs in 4-zone right layout

### Bug 1 — Missing InsertBefore Right@0
No code path adds the leftmost thin slot (before the Right zone) when all four right zones are occupied. All three conditional branches require at least one right zone to be empty or suppressed. This is the missing 5th thin slot the N+1 check flags.

### Bug 2 — Wrong semantic label on between-Right3/Right4 thin (line 404)
The thin drawn *between* Right3 and Right4 is labeled `InsertAfter Right4@1`. It should be `InsertBefore Right4@0`. Because the label says InsertAfter, hovering it shows the preview strip at the far-right edge of Right4 (x=2495) rather than the left edge of Right4. This is the wrong-position hover you're seeing.

### Bug 3 — Outer edge thin lands before Right4 instead of after it (line 418)
The `InsertAfter Right4` outer thin is added to `rightThinPositions` before `right4X = curX` is set and before `curX += right4ZoneWidth` is called. Both thins (bug 2 and bug 3) end up squeezed between Right3 and Right4, causing the adjacent-thin violation.

### Fix approach (loop-based, extensible)
A loop over an ordered list of `(zone, occupied, panelCount)` descriptors for each side:
- Emit `InsertBefore` thin at the **first** occupied zone's outer edge
- Emit inter-zone `InsertBefore` thin between **each adjacent pair** of occupied zones
- Emit `InsertAfter` thin **after the last** occupied zone (correct position by construction)

No zone-name checks needed. Handles 1–4 zones on either side identically. This is the existing backlog task: **[Docking] Refactor DockingMapBuilder to loop-based zone layout**.
